body:
## Implementation plan ready — BIN-92

I have reviewed the ticket, design brief, and codebase and written a full implementation plan.

**Plan summary:**
- 3 new files: `ManuscriptFindPanel` component + CSS module, `src/lib/findReplace.ts`
- 2 modified files: `src/lib/scenes.ts` (add `updateSceneFields`), `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` (keyboard shortcuts + panel render)
- Search across scene `title` and `summary` fields (the current data model)
- 300ms debounced search; grouped results by chapter → scene → match
- Session undo stack (capped at 50 entries)
- Replace All requires inline confirmation showing exact match count
- Keyboard-first: `Cmd/Ctrl+F` (scene scope), `Shift+Cmd/Ctrl+F` (manuscript scope), `Escape` to close
- Responsive: persistent sidebar on desktop, slide-over on tablet, full overlay on mobile
- All grayscale design tokens from `tokens.css`

Full plan is in the issue description.

Assign to: Code Monkey

## UX goal
Enable fast, low-risk manuscript-wide find/replace so writers can revise names/terms across many scenes with confidence.

## Information hierarchy
1. Query and scope controls.
2. Match count and grouped results.
3. Replace actions.
4. Undo visibility.

## Layout and components
- Use the existing editor sidebar area for a Find panel.
- Top: Find input, Replace input.
- Middle: scope toggles (scene/chapter/manuscript), case and whole-word toggles.
- Bottom: grouped results (chapter -> scene -> matches) with snippet, jump, replace, skip.

## Interaction states
- Idle: shortcut hint.
- Loading: skeleton result rows.
- Empty: no matches + clear filters.
- Error: inline panel error, keep query.
- Success: replaced count + Undo action.

## Accessibility
- Full keyboard access for panel controls and results.
- Strong visible focus on inputs, toggles, and active match row.
- Maintain readable contrast for primary and secondary text.
- Use explicit action labels (for example: Replace all 24 matches).

## Visual style
- Follow grayscale tokens only from src/styles/tokens.css.
- Primary text: --color-black-700.
- Secondary/meta: --color-elephant-500 and --color-cement-400.
- Borders/backgrounds: --color-fashion-300, --color-polar-100, --color-white.
- Hover/active row: --color-fog-200.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop: persistent right panel.
- Tablet: collapsible slide-over panel.
- Mobile: full-height overlay panel with sticky actions.

## Codebase alignment
Reviewed and aligned with:
- src/app/layout.tsx
- src/components/AppShell/AppShell.tsx
- src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx
- src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css
- src/components/Header/*, Navigation/*, ContentContainer/*, ViewModeSwitch/*
- src/styles/tokens.css

## Reuse vs new build
- Reuse: editor two-column layout, tokenized form styles, focus patterns.
- New: ManuscriptFindPanel, grouped result list, replace confirmation + session undo UI.

## Feasibility notes
- Debounce search and render groups incrementally for 100-scene performance.
- Replace-all must validate current match mapping at commit time.
- Undo requires session transaction state.

## Constraints / implementation notes
- Anchor UI in src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx where Notes placeholder is now.
- Keep panel scroll separate from editor scroll.
- Preserve keyboard shortcuts (Cmd/Ctrl+F and Shift+Cmd/Ctrl+F).
- Require explicit Replace All confirmation with exact match count.

## Handoff
Assign to: Founding Engineer

## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
Manuscript-wide find/replace is table stakes, but we can stand out by making bulk edits safer and more confidence-building than typical implementations: scoped filters, grouped previews, per-result skip, explicit confirmation, and session undo on large manuscripts.

## Competitor evidence
- Dabble documents project-level find/replace, with `Find in Project`, `Replace all/one`, and filters (`Match whole word`, `Match case`, regex): https://help.dabblewriter.com/en/articles/2865471-find-and-replace-in-dabble
- Campfire’s official team account confirms find/replace from the manuscript toolbar (search > expand): https://www.reddit.com/r/CampfireTechnology/comments/tv1acj/search_and_replace_feature/
- Inkitt’s editing guidance is chapter-by-chapter via story editor, not a manuscript-scale revision workflow: https://inkitt.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360011943299-How-do-I-edit-my-story and https://inkitt.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/360011943859-How-do-I-add-more-chapters-to-my-story

## Risks / trade-offs
- Replace-all can cause high-impact mistakes if preview/confirmation is weak.
- Cross-scene indexing can create latency on large manuscripts if search is not incremental/cached.
- Undo expectations are high; partial failure paths must be explicit.

## Recommendation
Approve. Position this as a “safe revision engine,” not just search parity: prioritize preview clarity, reversible operations, and reliable performance at scale.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design
